channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as host Jim Vokal rants about rising property taxes, the shell game elected officials are playing, and solutions Platte is advocating for this year. Listen now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Bus rapid transit was originally supposed to provide light-rail-like services...

The post TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget
item.description:
Over the last several months, Maine Policy Institute has repeatedly pointed out the many problems that exist in Governor Mills’ 2026 supplemental budget. Maine lawmakers had an opportunity to fix serious flaws in the budget. Instead, the Legislature’s Appropriations Committee has taken an already problematic proposal from Janet Mills, doubled down, and made it worse. […]

The post From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant
item.description:
The following appeared on April 5, 2026 in the Santa Fe New Mexican. The article also appeared in papers across New Mexico. I recently came across a concept called the Curley Effect. It has nothing to do with Larry and Moe. After some research, I learned the Curley Effect, coined by economists Edward L. Glaeser […]

The post Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Foropportunity] GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act
item.description:
The Stronger Workforce for America Act of 2026 includes an invaluable option for state flexibility, the Make America Skilled Again Grants. Georgia and other states need this option so they can design workforce and safety net systems that meet families' immediate needs while providing a clearer path to opportunity and self-sufficiency.

The post GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome William Beard, co-author of the Goldwater Institute’s report: Data Centers a Free Market Model for the Digital Future. During the episode William and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss what data centers are, why they matter, and what separates the states that are winning investment...

The post Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution
item.description:
The Maine Supreme Judicial Court has issued a unanimous advisory opinion in which the Court concluded that the Legislature’s attempt to expand ranked-choice voting (RCV) to general elections for governor, state senator, and state representative would violate the Maine Constitution.  Although the decision came quickly, the outcome closely tracks the concerns that surfaced during oral […]

The post Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job
item.description:
by Wells Winegar New Jersey just made a long overdue move. With Governor Mikie Sherrill signing legislation to lift the […]

The post A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] John Charles Submits Public Comments on ODOE’s “TIGHGER 2.0” Climate Gap Measures
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The Oregon Department of Energy and the Oregon...

The post John Charles Submits Public Comments on ODOE’s “TIGHGER 2.0” Climate Gap Measures appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions
item.description:
Idaho Gov. Brad Little on April 10 signed into law a Freedom Foundation-supported measure crafted to end taxpayer support for teachers' unions in the Gem State. 

The post Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Bethlehem Manor LLC v. City of Bethlehem: Fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court
item.description: Beacon is fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansans Need a Responsible Budget
item.description: The Kansas House and Senate have already passed the FY 2027 budget bill, House Bill 2513, and sent it to Governor Laura Kelly. Aside from some line-item vetoes and (failed) overrides, the topline is essentially the same. Supporters are calling it responsible because it trims the State General Fund budget by $189.2 million below a […]

item.title: [Empowerms] U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home
item.description:
U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home In a time of growing global instability, the role of American strength is back at the center of the conversation. In this episode, Grant sits down with Roger Wicker, Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee and Mississippi’s senior senator. From rebuilding America’s military strength to…

The post U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy
item.description:
by Megan Whitman, NJPI Jr. Fellow Walk down any main street in New Jersey and you’re looking at the heart […]

The post Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Your Property Tax Bill in 2030
item.description:
By 2030, many Wisconsin taxpayers could face thousands in new taxes, with costs falling heaviest on local taxpayers even under the most optimistic assumptions about new state aid. The interactive calculator we released makes these impacts concrete. Policymakers and voters should carefully weigh whether this path truly serves students and communities or simply expands government at the expense of hardworking Wisconsin families. 

The post Your Property Tax Bill in 2030 appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012
item.description:
Movers from Massachusetts to New Hampshire brought with them $5.95 billion in net adjusted gross income (AGI) from 2012-2023, IRS data show, representing a massive transfer of wealth from the high-tax Bay State to low-tax New Hampshire.  New Hampshire’s net personal income gain from Massachusetts movers grew tenfold from 2012-2019, then suddenly doubled to a […]

The post Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012 appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Mspolicy] AI & the Liberty Movement
item.description:
Amazon has just announced another multi-billion-dollar data center project in Mississippi — the latest in a flood of inward investment now pouring into our state. But here’s the thing worth reflecting on: even AI-related investments on this scale are only a fraction of what is flowing into data centers and AI infrastructure across the country. […]

The post AI & the Liberty Movement  appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The SAVE Act: What It Is, Why It Matters, and What Florida’s Experience Tells Us
item.description:
Natalie Gonzalez Election policy is once again at the epicenter of national conversations. Over the past several presidential...

The post The SAVE Act: What It Is, Why It Matters, and What Florida’s Experience Tells Us appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Kneecapping Conservatives, Protecting Bureaucrats: Vander Woude’s Lame Duck Legacy
item.description: As the 2026 legislative session reached its disappointing end, so too did Chairman John Vander Woude’s tenure in the Legislature. Rather than attempting to solidify his legacy as one of defense for children, families, and freedom, he seemingly spent his final days in the Capitol running interference for the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare, […]

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2026 Green Book
item.description: 2026 Kansas Green Book – Exploring the relationship between the size of government and economic growth

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Referendums on development could kill state’s growth
item.description:
Subjecting big development proposals to popular vote risks killing statewide economic growth, observers say in the wake of a successful effort by Port Washington data center opponents to give citizens the ability to nix the future use of a key financing tool.

The post Referendums on development could kill state’s growth appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children
item.description:
By Zach Freimark and Sarah Hitchcock America First Policy Institute On July 4, 2025, Congress passed, on a bipartisan vote, a package of reconciliations, commonly referred to as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. Included in the bill was a provision that created the Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC), which funds elementary and secondary education […]

The post Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Sine Die Lawmakers returned to Jackson expecting veto overrides, but what followed was one of the more unusual endings to a legislative session in recent memory. We unpack the final day’s twists, the political fallout, and close the book on the 2026 session.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Understanding spending and its growth under Gov. MLG
item.description:
A policy blogger named Maria Davidson recently started posting a series of charts contrasting the growth in state spending vs the growth in the same state’s population over the past decade (one for California is linked). I asked for a New Mexico version of her chart, but none was forthcoming, so I made my own. But then […]

The post Understanding spending and its growth under Gov. MLG appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism
item.description:
Americans are allowed to voluntarily contribute money to the U.S. Treasury’s “tax me more” fund. But not much money is being donated despite the false appeals to “patriotism” peddled by tax hikers. In calling for tax increases, affluent Democrats and progressive activists try to assert that Americans opposed to such tax hikes are lacking in […]

The post “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question
item.description:
In the fall, Maine voters will have the opportunity to vote on a citizen referendum aimed at protecting girls’ sports by requiring schools to separate athletic teams and certain facilities based on biological sex as recorded at birth. Before they ever get the chance to vote on the policy, however, there is a critically important […]

The post Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Mississippi Overtakes Britain
item.description:
Something remarkable has happened here in Mississippi. The state that for most of the last century sat at the bottom of nearly every American economic table has, quietly, pulled ahead of the United Kingdom in GDP per capita. Last week, Governor Tate Reeves highlighted the fact on X/Twitter in his characteristically Southern style - and […]

The post Mississippi Overtakes Britain appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Has a Cost Problem—And It’s Driving People Away
item.description: Kansas isn’t collapsing. That would at least get attention. What’s happening is quieter and more dangerous: the state is becoming too expensive, too fragmented, and too average to compete, while other states pull ahead. The new 2026 Kansas Green Book lays it out clearly. Kansas is not leading where it matters. It is lagging, and […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Adds New Deputy Counsel, Expands Litigation Capacity
item.description:
WILL is proud to announce the addition of Rebecca Furdek as Deputy Counsel. Her hire reflects WILL’s continued growth and commitment to litigating high-stakes cases promoting free speech, economic liberty, and equal rights.

The post WILL Adds New Deputy Counsel, Expands Litigation Capacity appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea
item.description:
It would run afoul of the First Amendment, among other issues. By Annette Meeks Guest contributor to the Minnesota Star Tribune April 20, 2026 ••• What do progressives do when they run out of other people’s money to spend? You don’t have to look any further than Minnesota to find out: Recently Gov. Tim Walz […]

The post Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] What Unions Were Meant to Be — And What They’ve Become, A Story of Self Destruction
item.description:
Unions were created with a simple mission to perform: Protecting workers. Standing in between employees and abuse.

The post What Unions Were Meant to Be — And What They’ve Become, A Story of Self Destruction appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Over Five Years Later, Arbeeny Family Support Still Critical to Promoting Government Transparency
item.description:
Albany, NY — The Arbeeny family and the Empire Center are marking five years of partnership in remembrance of Norman....

The post Over Five Years Later, Arbeeny Family Support Still Critical to Promoting Government Transparency appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Does San Francisco Need Another Union-Backed Tax?
item.description:
San Francisco and Portland are the only two US cities that tax companies if the pay ratio between the CEO and other employees is “too high.”. After agreeing to lower this tax in 2024 via Proposition M, the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) and its organized labor allies are pushing a ballot measure to raise...

The post Does San Francisco Need Another Union-Backed Tax? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Restricting Use, Reducing Supply: The Consequences of STR Bans for ADU Production
item.description:
Florida’s housing affordability crisis is no longer theoretical. Population growth continues to outpace homebuilding in Florida, while higher...

The post Restricting Use, Reducing Supply: The Consequences of STR Bans for ADU Production appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Measure of a Man
item.description: House Bill 461, introduced late in the 2025 legislative session, would have required the state to run an analysis of the Idaho Launch program to determine its effectiveness. I won’t bore you with all the details, but essentially the question is: “Does this program help the state the way Gov. Brad Little and other proponents […]

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Abundance Alliance
item.description:
Abundance, and its political twin, affordability, are now bipartisan mantras, but cannot be realized if the only permissible avenues are via urban infill, renewable energy, and water rationing. California is uniquely positioned to do much more. Breakthrough technologies and big projects, both pioneered here, could unite a powerful coalition of farmers, energy companies, high tech...

The post The Abundance Alliance first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] World Tour: Age Verification Edition
item.description:
Attempts to age gate the internet and restrict social media use based on age checks are not distinct to American state legislatures. Countries with less robust free speech protections have had an easier time passing these regulations at a large scale—with disastrous results. As it turns out, requiring all social media users in a nation...

The post World Tour: Age Verification Edition appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut AI Bill Would Put Workplace Technology on the Bargaining Table
item.description:
A bill headed to the Connecticut Senate floor would reshape how employers use artificial intelligence (AI) in the workplace — adding new regulatory requirements, compliance costs, and, in the public […]

The post Connecticut AI Bill Would Put Workplace Technology on the Bargaining Table  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Even open enrollment opponents say it will benefit students
item.description:
The debate over public school open enrollment is over. How do we know? Opponents have conceded the main point: Open enrollment benefits students.  Senate Bill 101, arguably the most important bill of the 2026 legislative session, would let students enroll in any New Hampshire public school that has room for them. Have a problem with […]

The post Even open enrollment opponents say it will benefit students appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Unmet Demand for a Well-Rounded Education WE STRESS the importance of teaching history in our newly published Indiana Mandate. The Founders believed the country would remain free only if its people learned history’s lessons. Benjamin Franklin noted that history would show the young “the advantages of liberty, mischiefs of licentiousness, benefits arising from good laws and Read the full article...

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Oregon Should Opt Into the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit So Private Giving Stays with Oregon Kids
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok Last summer, Congress passed the first federal-level legislation promoting educational...

The post Oregon Should Opt Into the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit So Private Giving Stays with Oregon Kids appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Why Iowa?
item.description:
From a public policy standpoint, Iowa has become a national leader in conservative reform. On taxes, budgeting, regulation, school choice, and protections for life and civil liberties, the state stands in sharp contrast to neighbors such as Minnesota and Illinois. But policy alone does not fully explain why so many people love Iowa. “What happened this year went beyond Iowa nice. You showed a humanity that rivals what most of us have seen in our lifetime,” stated Gov. Kim Reynolds in her 2021 Condition of the State Address. Reynolds was referencing the numerous humanitarian acts in response to the COVID pandemic and the devastating storms. Across Iowa, individuals, organizations and businesses stepped up to help one another and their communities during these crises.

The post Why Iowa? appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California Overestimated Deficits by $2 Billion and That’s Actually Good News
item.description:
Last week, KCRA 3 in Sacramento reported that the Newsom administration’s January budget contained a roughly $2 billion accounting error tied to CalPERS pension contributions. The nonpartisan Legislative Analyst’s Office flagged the problem in February. Legislative leaders knew. The public didn’t find out until April. The story is genuinely newsworthy, but not for the reason...

The post California Overestimated Deficits by $2 Billion and That’s Actually Good News first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish
item.description:
After completing the Raising Highly Capable Kids workshop as a parent, Shaneika Murphy now supports other parents as a program facilitator. Her sons volunteer with the program, too. Together, the Murphys are a shining example of how strong families build strong communities.

The post Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Economic Development Bill Includes a Key Provision for Streamlining the Review of Housing Development Applications
item.description:
In Massachusetts, the permitting process for new residential development often slows down housing production and adds unnecessary costs for builders. These costs are then passed onto buyers and tenants, helping to explain why Massachusetts has some of the most expensive housing in the country.  Most residential developments, from single-family...

The post Massachusetts Economic Development Bill Includes a Key Provision for Streamlining the Review of Housing Development Applications appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] For glimpse of a dismal Wisconsin future, just look at our Great Lakes neighbor
item.description:
Wisconsin and Ontario are both manufacturing centers with similar economic strengths and vulnerabilities. The Badger State should learn from Ontario’s mistakes.

The post For glimpse of a dismal Wisconsin future, just look at our Great Lakes neighbor appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Easy graders make real life harder
item.description:
Students learn less and earn less when they have less incentive to actually study in order to get top grades.

The post Easy graders make real life harder appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Price hikes tied to wind and solar
item.description: Regional electricity costs increase as ‘green’ generation falls short of promises

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Vallas: In police misconduct mess, trial lawyers holding taxpayers hostage
item.description:
Kim Foxx’s policy of issuing mass exonerations without case-by-case scrutiny has invited a flood of costly litigation.

The post Vallas: In police misconduct mess, trial lawyers holding taxpayers hostage appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Institute Victories for Liberty in 2026
item.description:
The Goldwater Institute’s 2026 is off to an extraordinary start. So far this year, we have worked with partners across..

The post Goldwater Institute Victories for Liberty in 2026 appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Can Fully Fund LA GATOR and Here’s How—It’s a Matter of Priorities
item.description:
Louisiana lawmakers debating whether to fully fund the LA GATOR education savings account program should begin with a simple fact: voters want the option funded, and the money is available. The real issue is not affordability—it is whether state leaders are willing to prioritize students and families over outdated funding structures and political spending habits....

The post Louisiana Can Fully Fund LA GATOR and Here’s How—It’s a Matter of Priorities appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Better Broadband Needs Better Permitting Rules
item.description:
In rural Utah, a broadband provider waited nearly three years for permission to repair an existing cable line...

The post Better Broadband Needs Better Permitting Rules appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Data Manager
item.description:
The Empire Center for Public Policy (EC) seeks a Communications and Marketing Manager to plan, oversee and execute the Center’s communications, marketing and outreach strategy and activities.

The post Data Manager appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Double standards in Pueblo 70: Union power, taxpayer dollars and selective outrage
item.description:
After weeks of intense public pressure, including loud demands from union supporters, the Pueblo County School District 70 board approved a generous extension of its collective bargaining agreement with the Pueblo County Education Association.

The post Double standards in Pueblo 70: Union power, taxpayer dollars and selective outrage appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] US public school spending hits $1 trillion; NH ranks 7th per-pupil
item.description:
Spending on K-12 public education in the United States passed the $1 trillion mark in the 2024 fiscal year, just-released data from the federal National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) reveal. “Time to update all mentions of the size of this sector,” commented the Edunomics Lab at Georgetown University’s McCourt School of Public Policy. “Revenues […]

The post US public school spending hits $1 trillion; NH ranks 7th per-pupil appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Proposal Would Limit Public Access to College Course Materials
item.description:
A bill moving through the Connecticut General Assembly would change how the public accesses course materials at taxpayer-funded colleges, raising questions about transparency in higher education.  House Bill 5550 would […]

The post Connecticut Proposal Would Limit Public Access to College Course Materials  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.description:
The Goldwater Institute’s 2026 is off to an extraordinary start. So far this year, we have worked with partners across..

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: New Mexico should take advantage of federal education tax credit
item.description:
The following appeared in the Santa Fe New Mexican on April 24, 2026. I read state Rep. Raymundo Lara’s recent article in The New Mexican with disbelief (“N.M. should reject ‘education freedom’ farce,” My View, April 10). In it, Lara argues against New Mexico’s participation in a federal school choice program created by Congress that offers taxpayers […]

The post Opinion piece: New Mexico should take advantage of federal education tax credit appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: “A conservative is someone who stands athwart history, yelling, ‘Stop,’ at a time when no one is inclined to do so, or to have much patience with those who so urge it.” ― William F. Buckley Assessing the Next Indy Mayor AS INDIANAPOLIS SLIDES into progressivism, the way to think about Democrats is not what candidates stand for but what Read the full article...

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan’s inflated teacher evals are failing students
item.description: State congratulates itself while students fall further behind

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois housing shortage prices many out of homeownership
item.description:
Property taxes and restrictive zoning are driving the state’s housing crisis. New laws could help spur more construction.

The post Illinois housing shortage prices many out of homeownership appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] From Atoms to Algorithms: Positioning Tennessee to be America’s Energy Leader
item.description: Tennessee stands at a critical juncture facing unprecedented demands on the state’s energy infrastructure. By leveraging key opportunities, Tennessee can not only meet its own energy needs but also cement its role as a national model for abundant energy generation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Positioning Tennessee to Be America’s Energy Leader
item.description: Tennessee’s rich nuclear history shows the state is a leader in nuclear energy and advanced technologies. With new technology and rising electricity demand, there are now opportunities for Tennessee to not only meet the energy needs of its growing population but also to be a model for the whole country in developing and deploying affordable, abundant energy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Latest Labor Bill Expands Risk for Employers
item.description:
The House is preparing to vote on a sweeping omnibus labor bill that, on paper, aims to strengthen worker protections and improve working conditions. In practice, business groups and industry […]

The post Connecticut’s Latest Labor Bill Expands Risk for Employers appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Casinos
item.description: by Mark Franke There are two ways to think about governmental incentives for new commercial enterprises. One is the high-level theoretical or philosophical one. In this way of thinking, the basic question is whether government even should be involved in what essentially are free enterprise activities.  The other is to accept that government, being government, Read the full article...

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Montana should join the flat tax revolution
item.description: Montana could continue to improve its economic outlook by building on its impressive income tax reforms by moving to a flat income tax rate.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Center for Intellectual Freedom adds to ideas in higher ed
item.description:
The most practical way to view the Center for Intellectual Freedom at the University of Iowa is that it allows a broader range of ideas to flourish on campus. Reasonable people can disagree, and the center offers students an opportunity to engage more deeply with the nation’s history while participating in thoughtful, open discussion of important issues. That is, after all, a key tenet of higher education.

The post Center for Intellectual Freedom adds to ideas in higher ed appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Major Los Angeles School District Becomes First to Implement Screen Time Policy
item.description:
Recently, I wrote about efforts aimed at limiting screen time for instructional use in schools. I outlined how parents in the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) were pushing for […]

The post Major Los Angeles School District Becomes First to Implement Screen Time Policy appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Mississippi Is No Longer a Punchline. It's Becoming a Case Study.
item.description:
ALEC's Rich States, Poor States index now has Mississippi at 24th for economic outlook. The trend lines, and the policy choices behind them, explain why. The latest Rich States, Poor States index has just been published by ALEC, and for anyone who cares about the direction of our state, it makes for encouraging reading. Mississippi […]

The post Mississippi Is No Longer a Punchline. It's Becoming a Case Study. appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Newsom’s $19 Million Ad Campaign Can’t Polish California’s Image
item.description:
It’s going to take a lot more than a $19 million advertising blitz to make people forget about California’s problems. Governor Gavin Newsom, who is known to love “big, hairy, audacious goals,” recently hired the communications powerhouse Edelman to rehab the Golden State’s image, which, in Newsom’s telling, has been marred by right-wing conspiracy theories. […]

The post Newsom’s $19 Million Ad Campaign Can’t Polish California’s Image appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.description:
Recent data from the Energy Information Administration and Empire Center for Public Policy show New York’s average residential electricity prices....

The post New York’s Electricity Prices 70 Percent Above the National Average appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children
item.description:
GCO and its partners offered 15 RHCK workshops at 12 locations in 2025. Participants took pre- and post-workshop surveys that illustrated the ways families had grown stronger throughout the program.

The post Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Inaugural US DataLabs Ranking Finds Alaska the State Most Dependent on Federal Funds, Idaho the Least
item.description:
Per-capita analysis reveals surprising exposure in Virginia and Massachusetts, offering a fuller picture of how deeply federal dollars shape state economies 

The post Inaugural US DataLabs Ranking Finds Alaska the State Most Dependent on Federal Funds, Idaho the Least appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Backlash growing as legal marijuana’s effects hit home
item.description:
Jennifer Galardi and Scott Yenor write for the Federalist about the impact of legalized marijuana. In a recent podcast, Andrew Huberman interviewed Dr. Natalie Crawford, a reproductive endocrinologist. She revealed marijuana use is “hugely detrimental” to sperm production and quality. It’s just the latest in years of research and experience debunking claims that recreational marijuana...

The post Backlash growing as legal marijuana’s effects hit home appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Democrats’ role in presidential assassination attempts
item.description:
David Catron writes for the American Spectator about the role of Democrats’ rhetoric in today’s political violence. As Jesus of Nazareth put it, “You shall know them by their fruits.” This brings us to the Democrat claim that violence has no place in politics. Former President Barack Obama echoed this on social media Sunday morning:...

The post Democrats’ role in presidential assassination attempts appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] It’s Groundhog Day all over again on free trade
item.description: There’s a better way

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Bay Area police oversight is in turmoil—and the real problem is inside the oversight system itself
item.description:
Civilian police oversight was built on a simple premise: internal police discipline was not enough on its own. Independent civilian review would add transparency, improve accountability, and strengthen public trust. That model now exists in San Francisco, Oakland, Berkeley, and San Jose. But its defining feature today is not success or failure in individual cases. […]

The post Bay Area police oversight is in turmoil—and the real problem is inside the oversight system itself appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Pritzker’s 57 tax and fee hikes cost Illinoisans $77 billion
item.description:
The governor has presided over $77 billion in increases while Illinois’ economic growth lags.

The post Pritzker’s 57 tax and fee hikes cost Illinoisans $77 billion appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Red states are not waiting for Congress to pass the SAVE America Act
item.description:
States like Florida and South Dakota are taking matters into their own hands. Congress should follow their lead and ensure consistent voting standards. President Donald Trump continues to prioritize the passage of the SAVE America Act, keeping election integrity at the forefront in Washington. However, states are not waiting for Congress to act. Across the...

The post Red states are not waiting for Congress to pass the SAVE America Act first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Making sure released inmates have state IDs is a step toward rehabilitation
item.description:
For most law-abiding citizens, identification is so routine we barely think about it. But without an ID, everyday tasks become major obstacles. You cannot easily apply for a job, secure housing, open a bank account or handle basic paperwork without the ability to verify your identity. For someone leaving incarceration and trying to reenter society...

The post Making sure released inmates have state IDs is a step toward rehabilitation first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] St. Louis Demographics and the Future of the Region with Ness Sandoval
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with J.S. Onésimo “Ness” Sandoval, demographer and professor in the Department of Sociology and Anthropology at Saint Louis University, about what the data says about the future […]

The post St. Louis Demographics and the Future of the Region with Ness Sandoval appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] A conversation with Governor Mitch Daniels
item.description: Mountain States Policy Center recently had the opportunity to have a conversation with former Indiana Governor Mitch Daniels to discuss his thoughts on spending discipline, health care reforms, improving education outcomes, higher education affordability, regulatory reforms, the national budget outlook, and civility.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Property Taxes Demand Real Reform in Iowa and Beyond
item.description:
Property tax reform is a national issue: while Tennessee’s effort failed due to local government opposition, Iowa still has a strong chance to pass meaningful limits this session. Double-digit property tax increases in both states are being driven by spending choices, not a lack of resources, with local governments often prioritizing discretionary projects over restraint. Warnings about cuts to essential services are overstated; without structural limits, taxpayers will continue to be squeezed and long-term economic competitiveness will suffer.

The post Property Taxes Demand Real Reform in Iowa and Beyond appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Enough Is Enough: The Left Must Distance Itself From Political Violence
item.description:
It’s happened again. There has been another attempt on the life of President Donald Trump. Was the alleged shooter politically motivated? It appears absolutely so. The “friendly federal assassin” is just the latest who seeks to fuse ideology and absurdity into their murderous fantasy. It was Saturday night when political pundits, members of Congress, cabinet...

The post Enough Is Enough: The Left Must Distance Itself From Political Violence first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Will Law] Court Grants WILL Motion to Dismiss, Dismisses 2nd Redistricting Case
item.description:
A three-judge panel today granted WILL’s motion to dismiss, ending a lawsuit brought against Wisconsin’s current congressional maps. 

The post Court Grants WILL Motion to Dismiss, Dismisses 2nd Redistricting Case appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Report: Off-Cycle Elections Cost Arizona Billions and Empower Special Interests
item.description:
On Election Day, millions of Americans turn out to vote for their representatives in state government, Congress, and the White..

The post Goldwater Report: Off-Cycle Elections Cost Arizona Billions and Empower Special Interests appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Bring out your dead! Elections boards to remove 34,000 deceased voters
item.description:
The North Carolina State Board of Elections (SBE) approved rules for removing non-citizens from voter rolls on a 3-2 party-line vote in April. Part of that process involves matching voter rolls with the Department of Homeland Security’s Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) database. While the SBE has long used the SAVE database, the new...

The post Bring out your dead! Elections boards to remove 34,000 deceased voters appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service
item.description:
Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service Mississippi Secretary of State Michael Watson returns to the Empower Podcast as he launches his campaign for Lt. Governor. In this conversation, Grant sits down with Watson for a more personal look at the experiences and convictions that have shaped his leadership. From growing up on the Gulf…

The post Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Rules of the Road: Driverless Cars and Federal Frameworks
item.description:
Self-driving cars took to New Orleans last year to learn the roads, pot holes, and all of the city’s idiosyncrasies. These “robotaxis,” an AI-powered product of Waymo, have collected some impressive numbers and are proving to be significantly safer than cars driven by humans. The promise of reduced accidents and the secondary benefit of increased tech sector activity...

The post Rules of the Road: Driverless Cars and Federal Frameworks appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Breaking Up ‘Big Medicine’ Won’t Fix What Washington Broke
item.description:
Washington is gearing up to crack down on “Big Medicine,” with populist Sens. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and Josh Hawley, R-Mo., leading the charge. Over the past decade, America’s healthcare system has become increasingly consolidated, leaving patients with higher prices, fewer choices, and more bureaucratic frustration. But before lawmakers swing a scythe through the healthcare sector, […]

The post Breaking Up ‘Big Medicine’ Won’t Fix What Washington Broke appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again
item.description:
New Jersey should opt into the Education Freedom Tax Credit. The union’s opposition tells you everything about whose interests it […]

The post The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] They say Idaho spends the least per student... does it really matter?
item.description: The real issue isn’t that Idaho spends too little—it’s whether we’re getting results for what we spend. And right now, the data suggests something important: We can do better without simply spending more.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] The Sooner State joins the Southern Surge
item.description:
Oklahoma has just passed a comprehensive early literacy reform package that mimics the “best in class” legislation of the Southern Surge. The term Southern Surge denotes recent significant educational progress, particularly in early literacy, made by Southern states like Mississippi, Louisiana, and Alabama. In particular, Mississippi’s early literacy reform package, which includes teacher training, “science of...

The post The Sooner State joins the Southern Surge appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Kimmel CRINGE: Walz’s late night LIES
item.description:
Fresh off a book deal and PAC launch, Governor Walz joined Jimmy Kimmel to downplay the fraud scandal and rip President Trump (what else is new?), which warrants an edition of STOP THE TAPE. The Minneapolis Public School district is facing a self-inflicted $75 million budget deficit and somehow, the story keeps getting more absurd....

The post Kimmel CRINGE: Walz’s late night LIES appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Nuclear Energy and Construction Works in Progress (CWIP)
item.description:
Last year, the passage of Senate Bill (SB) 4 allowed natural gas plants to raise rates to pay for construction before plants are put into operation, a process known as […]

The post Nuclear Energy and Construction Works in Progress (CWIP) appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Did Aimee Bock leak to the Star Tribune?
item.description:
The U.S. Dept. of Justice has accused Aimee Bock and her adult sons of sending confidential court material to the Minnesota Star Tribune and other media outlets. Bock, defendant No. 1 in the sprawling free-food scandal, is scheduled to be sentenced on May 21, following her courtroom conviction last year. Bock was the founder and...

The post Did Aimee Bock leak to the Star Tribune? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Buried Without a Vote: The Secret Power of Committee Chairmen
item.description: It isn’t a power afforded in House or Senate rules. It isn’t written into Mason’s Manual of Legislative Procedure, and it certainly isn’t penned into the Constitution. Yet, during legislative sessions at the Idaho Capitol, it is utilized and treated as an authorized exercise of power.  We are, of course, talking about the chairman’s drawer. […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] New Study Finds Massachusetts Business Formation Has Plummeted Despite National Surge
item.description:
The Commonwealth had the nation’s lowest net business formation rate from 2020 to 2024, losing more than 17,000 employer businesses over nine consecutive quarters 

The post New Study Finds Massachusetts Business Formation Has Plummeted Despite National Surge appeared first on Pioneer Institute.
